subject: Physics
title: The Cavendish Experiment
description: Learn The Cavendish Experiment in one minute.

hook.text: In 1798, Henry Cavendish weighed the entire Earth using two lead balls and a wooden beam.

buildup.text: He placed heavy lead spheres near smaller ones and measured the tiny gravitational pull between them.

discovery.text: The attraction was incredibly small but measurable. It revealed the gravitational constant, G.

twist.text: With G known, he could calculate Earth's mass: about six trillion trillion kilograms.

climax.text: His result was within 1% of today's accepted value—astonishing for 1798 technology.

punchline.text: Two lead balls in a shed. That's how we weighed a planet.

quiz.question: What did the Cavendish experiment ultimately allow scientists to calculate?
options: The age of the solar system
